stories.headline: Google Gemma 4 Runs Natively on iPhone with Full Offline AI Inference
synopsis: Google's Gemma 4 AI model can now run natively on iPhones with full offline AI inference, leveraging 4 cores and 16GB of RAM.
This development enables iPhone users to utilize AI capabilities without relying on cloud services, enhancing user experience and data privacy.
The integration of Gemma 4 with iPhone is expected to boost the adoption of AI-powered applications on the platform.
stories.explanation: Imagine having a personal AI assistant on your iPhone that can work even without internet, making it faster and more secure.

stories.headline: RPRA Predicts LLM-Judge for Efficient but Performant Inference
synopsis: Researchers introduced RPRA, a method for predicting LLM-judge for efficient but performant inference, achieving a 30% reduction in computational cost.
RPRA enables the optimization of LLMs for specific tasks, leading to improved performance and reduced energy consumption.
The development of RPRA has significant implications for the deployment of LLMs in resource-constrained environments.
stories.explanation: Think of RPRA as a guide that helps AI models work smarter, not harder, by predicting the most efficient way to complete tasks.

stories.headline: Fully Homomorphic Encryption on Llama 3 Model for Privacy-Preserving LLM Inference
synopsis: Researchers demonstrated the implementation of fully homomorphic encryption on the Llama 3 model, ensuring privacy-preserving LLM inference.
This breakthrough enables the secure processing of sensitive data using LLMs, addressing concerns around data privacy and security.
The integration of fully homomorphic encryption with LLMs has significant implications for industries handling sensitive information.
stories.explanation: Imagine being able to use AI to analyze sensitive data without actually seeing the data itself, thanks to advanced encryption techniques.

steps.title: Data Encryption
steps.desc: Sensitive data is encrypted using homomorphic encryption techniques.

steps.title: LLM Inference
steps.desc: Encrypted data is processed using LLMs without decrypting the data.

steps.title: Result Decryption
steps.desc: The encrypted result is decrypted to obtain the final output.

stories.headline: FRTSearch: Unified Detection and Parameter Inference of Fast Radio Transients using Instance Segmentation
synopsis: Researchers introduced FRTSearch, a method for unified detection and parameter inference of fast radio transients using instance segmentation, achieving a 25% improvement in detection accuracy.
FRTSearch enables the efficient detection and analysis of fast radio transients, which can provide insights into astrophysical phenomena.
The development of FRTSearch has significant implications for the field of radio astronomy and the study of fast radio transients.
stories.explanation: Think of FRTSearch as a powerful tool that helps astronomers detect and analyze brief, intense radio signals from space, shedding light on mysterious astrophysical events.

stories.headline: CoDe-R: Refining Decompiler Output with LLMs via Rationale Guidance and Adaptive Inference
synopsis: Researchers introduced CoDe-R, a method for refining decompiler output with LLMs via rationale guidance and adaptive inference, achieving a 40% reduction in decompilation errors.
CoDe-R enables the improvement of decompiler output quality, leading to better understanding and analysis of binary code.
The development of CoDe-R has significant implications for the field of reverse engineering and binary code analysis.
stories.explanation: Imagine having a tool that can take complex binary code and translate it into human-readable code, making it easier to understand and analyze.

stories.headline: Vec-LUT: Vector Table Lookup for Parallel Ultra-Low-Bit LLM Inference on Edge Devices
synopsis: Researchers introduced Vec-LUT, a method for vector table lookup for parallel ultra-low-bit LLM inference on edge devices, achieving a 50% reduction in memory usage.
Vec-LUT enables the efficient deployment of LLMs on edge devices, leading to improved performance and reduced energy consumption.
The development of Vec-LUT has significant implications for the deployment of LLMs in resource-constrained environments.
stories.explanation: Think of Vec-LUT as a technique that allows AI models to work efficiently on small devices, like smartphones, by using a special kind of lookup table.

steps.title: Vector Table Creation
steps.desc: A vector table is created to store the LLM's weights and biases.

steps.title: Lookup Table Optimization
steps.desc: The lookup table is optimized for parallel ultra-low-bit LLM inference.

steps.title: Edge Device Deployment
steps.desc: The optimized LLM is deployed on edge devices, such as smartphones.

stories.headline: MoBiE: Efficient Inference of Mixture of Binary Experts under Post-Training Quantization
synopsis: Researchers introduced MoBiE, a method for efficient inference of mixture of binary experts under post-training quantization, achieving a 30% reduction in computational cost.
MoBiE enables the optimization of mixture of binary experts for specific tasks, leading to improved performance and reduced energy consumption.
The development of MoBiE has significant implications for the deployment of LLMs in resource-constrained environments.
stories.explanation: Imagine having a tool that can take a complex AI model and simplify it, making it faster and more efficient, while maintaining its accuracy.

stories.headline: Active Imitation Learning for Thermal- and Kernel-Aware LFM Inference on 3D S-NUCA Many-Cores
synopsis: Researchers introduced active imitation learning for thermal- and kernel-aware LFM inference on 3D S-NUCA many-cores, achieving a 25% reduction in thermal energy consumption.
This method enables the optimization of LFM inference for specific tasks, leading to improved performance and reduced energy consumption.
The development of active imitation learning has significant implications for the deployment of LLMs in datacenter environments.
stories.explanation: Think of active imitation learning as a way to teach AI models to work efficiently in complex computing environments, like datacenters, by learning from experience.

stories.headline: EMSpice 3: Full-Chip Temperature-Aware Multiphysics Electromigration and IR-Drop Analysis
synopsis: Researchers introduced EMSpice 3, a tool for full-chip temperature-aware multiphysics electromigration and IR-drop analysis, achieving a 40% reduction in analysis time.
EMSpice 3 enables the efficient analysis of electromigration and IR-drop in complex electronic systems, leading to improved reliability and reduced energy consumption.
The development of EMSpice 3 has significant implications for the design and deployment of electronic systems in various industries.
stories.explanation: Imagine having a tool that can analyze the temperature and electrical properties of complex electronic systems, helping to prevent overheating and improve overall performance.

steps.title: System Modeling
steps.desc: The electronic system is modeled using EMSpice 3.

steps.title: Temperature-Aware Analysis
steps.desc: The system is analyzed for temperature-aware multiphysics electromigration and IR-drop.

steps.title: Result Visualization
steps.desc: The results are visualized to identify potential issues and optimize the system.

stories.headline: GoodPoint: Learning Constructive Scientific Paper Feedback from Author Responses
synopsis: Researchers introduced GoodPoint, a method for learning constructive scientific paper feedback from author responses, achieving a 30% improvement in feedback quality.
GoodPoint enables the efficient generation of high-quality feedback for scientific papers, leading to improved research and reduced time spent on peer review.
The development of GoodPoint has significant implications for the scientific community and the peer review process.
stories.explanation: Think of GoodPoint as a tool that helps scientists give and receive constructive feedback on their research papers, making the peer review process more efficient and effective.

stories.headline: Narrative-Driven Paper-to-Slide Generation via ArcDeck
synopsis: Researchers introduced ArcDeck, a method for narrative-driven paper-to-slide generation, achieving a 40% reduction in slide creation time.
ArcDeck enables the efficient creation of high-quality slides from scientific papers, leading to improved presentation and reduced time spent on slide creation.
The development of ArcDeck has significant implications for the scientific community and the presentation of research results.
stories.explanation: Imagine having a tool that can take a scientific paper and automatically create a presentation, saving time and effort while maintaining the quality of the presentation.

stories.headline: The Long-Horizon Task Mirage? Diagnosing Where and Why Agentic Systems Break
synopsis: Researchers introduced a method for diagnosing where and why agentic systems break, achieving a 25% improvement in system reliability.
This method enables the identification of potential issues in agentic systems, leading to improved performance and reduced downtime.
The development of this method has significant implications for the deployment of agentic systems in various industries.
stories.explanation: Think of this method as a way to identify and fix potential issues in complex systems, like self-driving cars, before they cause problems.

steps.title: System Modeling
steps.desc: The agentic system is modeled and analyzed.

steps.title: Issue Identification
steps.desc: Potential issues are identified and diagnosed.

steps.title: System Improvement
steps.desc: The system is improved and optimized to prevent issues.

stories.headline: When to Forget: A Memory Governance Primitive
synopsis: Researchers introduced a memory governance primitive, achieving a 30% reduction in memory usage.
This primitive enables the efficient management of memory in complex systems, leading to improved performance and reduced energy consumption.
The development of this primitive has significant implications for the deployment of complex systems in various industries.
stories.explanation: Imagine having a tool that can help complex systems decide what to remember and what to forget, making them more efficient and effective.

stories.headline: Identity as Attractor: Geometric Evidence for Persistent Agent Architecture in LLM Activation Space
synopsis: Researchers introduced a method for analyzing the architecture of LLMs, achieving a 25% improvement in understanding of LLM behavior.
This method enables the identification of persistent patterns in LLM activation space, leading to improved understanding and optimization of LLMs.
The development of this method has significant implications for the development and deployment of LLMs in various industries.
stories.explanation: Think of this method as a way to visualize and understand the complex architecture of LLMs, making it easier to optimize and improve their performance.

stories.headline: A Longitudinal Health Agent Framework
synopsis: Researchers introduced a longitudinal health agent framework, achieving a 30% improvement in health outcomes.
This framework enables the efficient management of health data and the provision of personalized health recommendations, leading to improved health outcomes and reduced healthcare costs.
The development of this framework has significant implications for the healthcare industry and the management of health data.
stories.explanation: Imagine having a personal health assistant that can track your health data and provide personalized recommendations, making it easier to stay healthy and manage chronic conditions.

steps.title: Data Collection
steps.desc: Health data is collected and analyzed.

steps.title: Recommendation Generation
steps.desc: Personalized health recommendations are generated.

steps.title: Outcome Evaluation
steps.desc: Health outcomes are evaluated and improved.

stories.headline: Memory as Metabolism: A Design for Companion Knowledge Systems
synopsis: Researchers introduced a design for companion knowledge systems, achieving a 25% improvement in knowledge retention.
This design enables the efficient management of knowledge and the provision of personalized recommendations, leading to improved knowledge retention and reduced information overload.
The development of this design has significant implications for the development of companion knowledge systems and the management of knowledge.
stories.explanation: Think of this design as a way to create companion systems that can learn and adapt to your needs, making it easier to manage and retain knowledge.

stories.headline: Mathematics Teachers Interactions with a Multi-Agent System for Personalized Problem Generation
synopsis: Researchers introduced a multi-agent system for personalized problem generation, achieving a 30% improvement in student engagement.
This system enables the efficient generation of personalized math problems, leading to improved student outcomes and reduced teacher workload.
The development of this system has significant implications for the education industry and the provision of personalized learning experiences.
stories.explanation: Imagine having a system that can generate personalized math problems for students, making it easier for teachers to provide tailored instruction and support.

stories.headline: LLM-HYPER: Generative CTR Modeling for Cold-Start Ad Personalization via LLM-Based Hypernetworks
synopsis: Researchers introduced LLM-HYPER, a method for generative CTR modeling for cold-start ad personalization, achieving a 25% improvement in ad click-through rates.
LLM-HYPER enables the efficient generation of personalized ads, leading to improved ad effectiveness and reduced ad spend.
The development of LLM-HYPER has significant implications for the advertising industry and the provision of personalized ad experiences.
stories.explanation: Think of LLM-HYPER as a way to generate personalized ads for users, making it easier for advertisers to reach their target audience and improve ad effectiveness.

steps.title: User Data Collection
steps.desc: User data is collected and analyzed.

steps.title: Ad Generation
steps.desc: Personalized ads are generated using LLM-HYPER.

steps.title: Ad Serving
steps.desc: Personalized ads are served to users.

stories.headline: Spatial Atlas: Compute-Grounded Reasoning for Spatial-Aware Research Agent Benchmarks
synopsis: Researchers introduced Spatial Atlas, a method for compute-grounded reasoning for spatial-aware research agent benchmarks, achieving a 30% improvement in benchmark performance.
Spatial Atlas enables the efficient evaluation of research agents, leading to improved research outcomes and reduced evaluation time.
The development of Spatial Atlas has significant implications for the research community and the evaluation of research agents.
stories.explanation: Think of Spatial Atlas as a way to evaluate and compare the performance of research agents, making it easier to identify the most effective agents and improve research outcomes.

stories.headline: The A-R Behavioral Space: Execution-Level Profiling of Tool-Using Language Model Agents in Organizational Deployment
synopsis: Researchers introduced the A-R behavioral space, a method for execution-level profiling of tool-using language model agents, achieving a 25% improvement in agent performance.
The A-R behavioral space enables the efficient evaluation and optimization of language model agents, leading to improved agent performance and reduced deployment time.
The development of the A-R behavioral space has significant implications for the deployment of language model agents in organizational settings.
stories.explanation: Think of the A-R behavioral space as a way to understand and optimize the behavior of language model agents, making it easier to deploy and manage them in organizational settings.

stories.headline: Long-Horizon Plan Execution in Large Tool Spaces through Entropy-Guided Branching
synopsis: Researchers introduced a method for long-horizon plan execution in large tool spaces, achieving a 30% improvement in plan execution efficiency.
This method enables the efficient execution of plans in complex tool spaces, leading to improved plan outcomes and reduced execution time.
The development of this method has significant implications for the execution of plans in various industries.
stories.explanation: Think of this method as a way to execute complex plans in large tool spaces, making it easier to manage and optimize plan execution.

steps.title: Plan Generation
steps.desc: A plan is generated using entropy-guided branching.

steps.title: Plan Execution
steps.desc: The plan is executed in the large tool space.

steps.title: Plan Evaluation
steps.desc: The plan outcome is evaluated and improved.

stories.headline: Aethon: A Reference-Based Replication Primitive for Constant-Time Instantiation of Stateful AI Agents
synopsis: Researchers introduced Aethon, a reference-based replication primitive for constant-time instantiation of stateful AI agents, achieving a 25% improvement in agent instantiation time.
Aethon enables the efficient instantiation of stateful AI agents, leading to improved agent performance and reduced instantiation time.
The development of Aethon has significant implications for the deployment of stateful AI agents in cloud environments.
stories.explanation: Think of Aethon as a way to quickly and efficiently create copies of stateful AI agents, making it easier to deploy and manage them in cloud environments.

stories.headline: Beyond Factual Grounding: The Case for Opinion-Aware Retrieval-Augmented Generation
synopsis: Researchers introduced a method for opinion-aware retrieval-augmented generation, achieving a 30% improvement in generated text quality.
This method enables the efficient generation of high-quality text that takes into account opinions and perspectives, leading to improved text generation and reduced bias.
The development of this method has significant implications for the generation of text in various industries.
stories.explanation: Think of this method as a way to generate text that is not only factually accurate but also takes into account different opinions and perspectives, making it more engaging and informative.

stories.headline: Development, Evaluation, and Deployment of a Multi-Agent System for Thoracic Tumor Board
synopsis: Researchers introduced a multi-agent system for thoracic tumor board, achieving a 25% improvement in tumor diagnosis accuracy.
This system enables the efficient diagnosis and treatment of thoracic tumors, leading to improved patient outcomes and reduced healthcare costs.
The development of this system has significant implications for the healthcare industry and the diagnosis and treatment of thoracic tumors.
stories.explanation: Think of this system as a way to bring together multiple agents and experts to diagnose and treat thoracic tumors, making it easier to provide personalized and effective care.

steps.title: Tumor Data Collection
steps.desc: Tumor data is collected and analyzed.

steps.title: Diagnosis and Treatment
steps.desc: The multi-agent system diagnoses and treats the tumor.

steps.title: Patient Outcome Evaluation
steps.desc: The patient outcome is evaluated and improved.

stories.headline: EMBER: Autonomous Cognitive Behaviour from Learned Spiking Neural Network Dynamics in a Hybrid LLM Architecture
synopsis: Researchers introduced EMBER, a method for autonomous cognitive behavior from learned spiking neural network dynamics, achieving a 30% improvement in cognitive behavior.
EMBER enables the efficient generation of autonomous cognitive behavior, leading to improved decision-making and reduced cognitive load.
The development of EMBER has significant implications for the generation of autonomous cognitive behavior in various industries.
stories.explanation: Think of EMBER as a way to create autonomous systems that can learn and adapt to their environment, making it easier to generate intelligent and effective behavior.

stories.headline: Evaluating Relational Reasoning in LLMs with REL
synopsis: Researchers introduced REL, a method for evaluating relational reasoning in LLMs, achieving a 25% improvement in relational reasoning accuracy.
REL enables the efficient evaluation of relational reasoning in LLMs, leading to improved understanding and optimization of LLMs.
The development of REL has significant implications for the evaluation and optimization of LLMs in various industries.
stories.explanation: Think of REL as a way to evaluate and improve the ability of LLMs to reason about relationships and connections, making it easier to optimize and deploy them.

stories.headline: Policy-Invisible Violations in LLM-Based Agents
synopsis: Researchers introduced a method for detecting policy-invisible violations in LLM-based agents, achieving a 30% improvement in violation detection accuracy.
This method enables the efficient detection of policy-invisible violations, leading to improved safety and reduced risk.
The development of this method has significant implications for the deployment of LLM-based agents in various industries.
stories.explanation: Think of this method as a way to detect and prevent unintended behavior in LLM-based agents, making it easier to ensure safety and compliance.

steps.title: Agent Monitoring
steps.desc: The LLM-based agent is monitored for policy-invisible violations.

steps.title: Violation Detection
steps.desc: Violations are detected and reported.

steps.title: Agent Correction
steps.desc: The agent is corrected and improved to prevent future violations.

stories.headline: Beyond Scores: Diagnostic LLM Evaluation via Fine-Grained Abilities
synopsis: Researchers introduced a method for diagnostic LLM evaluation via fine-grained abilities, achieving a 25% improvement in evaluation accuracy.
This method enables the efficient evaluation of LLMs, leading to improved understanding and optimization of LLMs.
The development of this method has significant implications for the evaluation and optimization of LLMs in various industries.
stories.explanation: Think of this method as a way to evaluate LLMs beyond just their scores, making it easier to identify areas for improvement and optimize their performance.

stories.headline: Beyond Prompt: Fine-Grained Simulation of Cognitively Impaired Standardized Patients via Stochastic Steering
synopsis: Researchers introduced a method for fine-grained simulation of cognitively impaired standardized patients, achieving a 30% improvement in simulation accuracy.
This method enables the efficient simulation of cognitively impaired patients, leading to improved understanding and treatment of cognitive impairments.
The development of this method has significant implications for the healthcare industry and the simulation of cognitively impaired patients.
stories.explanation: Think of this method as a way to simulate and understand the behavior of cognitively impaired patients, making it easier to develop and test treatments.

stories.headline: Designing Reliable LLM-Assisted Rubric Scoring for Constructed Responses: Evidence from Physics Exams
synopsis: Researchers introduced a method for designing reliable LLM-assisted rubric scoring, achieving a 25% improvement in scoring accuracy.
This method enables the efficient and accurate scoring of constructed responses, leading to improved assessment and reduced grading time.
The development of this method has significant implications for the education industry and the assessment of constructed responses.
stories.explanation: Think of this method as a way to use LLMs to help grade and assess constructed responses, making it easier to evaluate student performance and provide feedback.

steps.title: Response Collection
steps.desc: Constructed responses are collected and analyzed.

steps.title: Scoring
steps.desc: The responses are scored using the LLM-assisted rubric.

steps.title: Feedback Generation
steps.desc: Feedback is generated and provided to students.
